title.zh: 卷二十一 任李萬邳劉耿列傳
title.en: Volume 21: Biographies of Ren, Li, Wan, Pi, Liu, Geng
title.raw: 卷二十一 任李萬邳劉耿列傳 Volume 21: Biographies of Ren, Li, Wan, Pi, Liu, Geng

sentences.zh: 任光字伯卿，南陽宛人也。

translations.literal: Ren Guang, courtesy name Boqing, was a native of Wan in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Guang, styled Boqing, came from Wan in Nanyang commandery.

sentences.zh: 少忠厚，為鄉裏所愛。

translations.literal: In youth he was honest and loyal, and was loved by his village and district.
translations.idiomatic: From boyhood he was known for honesty and loyalty, and his neighbors held him in affection.

sentences.zh: 初為鄉嗇夫、郡縣吏。

translations.literal: At first he served as village sefu and as clerk in the commandery and county.
translations.idiomatic: He began his career as a village overseer and then as a clerk in the commandery and county offices.

sentences.zh: 漢兵至宛，軍人見光冠服鮮明，令解衣，將殺而奪之。

translations.literal: When Han troops reached Wan, soldiers saw that Guang's cap and garments were bright and fine; they ordered him to strip, intending to kill him and seize them.
translations.idiomatic: When imperial forces reached Wan, some soldiers spotted Ren Guang's fine hat and robes, ordered him to undress, and were about to kill him for the plunder.

sentences.zh: 會光祿勛劉賜適至，視光容貌長者，乃救全之。

translations.literal: It happened that Superintendent of the Household Liu Ci arrived, looked at Guang's bearing as that of an elder man, and thereupon saved him and kept him whole.
translations.idiomatic: Superintendent of the Household Liu Ci happened to arrive, took one look at Ren Guang's dignified bearing, and intervened to spare his life.

sentences.zh: 光因率黨與從賜，為安集掾，拜偏將軍，與世祖破王尋、王邑。

translations.literal: Guang thereupon led his clique and followers to join Ci, served as an Anji adjutant, was appointed lieutenant general, and with the Shizu defeated Wang Xun and Wang Yi.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Guang then brought his followers to Liu Ci's service as an adjutant for pacification, was commissioned lieutenant general, and fought beside Emperor Guangwu to defeat Wang Xun and Wang Yi.

sentences.zh: 更始至洛陽，以光為信都太守。

translations.literal: When Gengshi reached Luoyang, he appointed Guang governor of Xindu.
translations.idiomatic: After the Gengshi Emperor entered Luoyang, he named Ren Guang governor of Xindu.

sentences.zh: 及王郎起，郡國皆降之，光獨不肯，遂與都尉李忠、令萬脩、功曹阮況、五官掾郭唐等同心固守。

translations.literal: When Wang Lang rose, commanderies and kingdoms all submitted to him; Guang alone refused, and with Commandant Li Zhong, Magistrate Wan Xiu, merit officer Ruan Kuang, and registrar Guo Tang and others he held fast with one mind.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Lang rebelled, every district around them capitulated; Ren Guang alone held out, standing shoulder to shoulder with Commandant Li Zhong, Magistrate Wan Xiu, merit officer Ruan Kuang, registrar Guo Tang, and the rest to defend the city.

sentences.zh: 廷掾持王郎檄詣府白光，光斬之於市，以徇百姓，發精兵四千人城守。

translations.literal: A court clerk brought Wang Lang's proclamation to the office to show Guang; Guang executed him in the marketplace to display to the common people, and raised four thousand picked troops to man the walls.
translations.idiomatic: A clerk arrived at headquarters with Wang Lang's summons; Ren Guang had him beheaded in the market as a warning to the people, then mobilized four thousand elite soldiers to hold the walls.

sentences.zh: 更始二年春，世祖自薊還，狼狽不知所向，傳聞信都獨為漢拒邯鄲，即馳赴之。

translations.literal: In the spring of the second year of Gengshi, the Shizu returned from Ji in disarray, not knowing where to turn; rumor said Xindu alone for Han resisted Handan, and he galloped thither.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of Gengshi 2, Emperor Guangwu was retreating in disorder from Ji with nowhere clear to go; word reached him that Xindu alone still held for Han against Handan, and he rode for it at once.

sentences.zh: 光等孤城獨守，恐不能全，聞世祖至，大喜，吏民皆稱萬歲，即時開門，與李忠、萬脩率官屬迎謁。

translations.literal: Guang and the others held an isolated city alone and feared they could not preserve it; when they heard the Shizu had come they rejoiced greatly, officials and people all cried "Wan sui," and at once opened the gates, and with Li Zhong and Wan Xiu led their subordinates to welcome him.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Guang and his comrades had been defending a lone city and feared they could not last; when they learned Guangwu had arrived, they were elated. Officials and commoners shouted long life, threw open the gates, and with Li Zhong and Wan Xiu went out in formal procession to receive him.

sentences.zh: 世祖入傳舍，謂光曰：『伯卿，今勢力虛弱，欲俱入城頭子路、力子都兵中，何如邪？

translations.literal: The Shizu entered the relay inn and said to Guang: "Boqing, our strength is now weak; I wish to join together the troops of Chengtou Zilu and Lizi Du in the city—what of it?"
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Guangwu went to the post station and said to Ren Guang, "Boqing, we are weak now. I am thinking of throwing in with Chengtou Zilu's and Lizi Du's bands in the city—what do you think?"

sentences.zh: 』光曰：『不可。

translations.literal: Guang said: "It cannot be done."
translations.idiomatic: Ren Guang replied, "That will not do."

sentences.zh: 』世祖曰：『卿兵少，如何？

translations.literal: The Shizu said: "Your troops are few—what then?"
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu asked, "Then what? Your force is small?"

sentences.zh: 』光曰：『可募發奔命，出攻傍縣，若不降者，恣聽掠之。

translations.literal: Guang said: "We may levy emergency runners, go out and strike neighboring counties, and as for those who will not submit, let them plunder at will."
translations.idiomatic: Ren Guang said, "Call up emergency levies, strike the neighboring counties, and tell the men they may loot freely wherever the enemy refuses to yield."

sentences.zh: 人貪財物，則兵可招而致也。

translations.literal: If people covet goods and wealth, then troops can be summoned and brought in.
translations.idiomatic: Once greed for plunder takes hold, you can draw soldiers to your banner."

sentences.zh: 』世祖從之。

translations.literal: The Shizu followed this.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Guangwu accepted the plan.

sentences.zh: 拜光為左大將軍，封武成侯，留南陽宗廣領信都太守事，使光將兵從。

translations.literal: He appointed Guang left grand general, enfeoffed him as marquis of Wucheng, left Zong Guang of Nanyang to handle the affairs of governor of Xindu, and ordered Guang to lead troops and follow.
translations.idiomatic: He named Ren Guang left grand general and marquis of Wucheng, left Zong Guang of Nanyang acting governor of Xindu, and took Ren Guang with him in the field.

sentences.zh: 光乃多作檄文曰：『大司馬劉公將城頭子路、力子都兵百萬眾從東方來，擊諸反虜。

translations.literal: Guang then made many proclamations saying: "Grand Marshal Liu is about to lead the troops of Chengtou Zilu and Lizi Du, a million strong, from the east to strike all the rebels."
translations.idiomatic: Ren Guang then had broadsheets forged in large numbers: "Grand Marshal Liu is leading Chengtou Zilu's and Lizi Du's hosts—a million men—from the east to crush the rebels."

sentences.zh: 』遣騎馳至巨鹿界中。

translations.literal: He sent riders galloping into the borders of Julu.
translations.idiomatic: He sent horsemen racing into the Julu area with the notices.

sentences.zh: 吏民得檄，傳相告語。

translations.literal: Officials and people who obtained the proclamations passed word to one another.
translations.idiomatic: Officials and townsfolk who saw the handbills spread the word from mouth to mouth.

sentences.zh: 世祖遂與光等投暮入堂陽界，使騎各持炬火，彌滿澤中，光炎燭天地，舉城莫不震驚惶怖，其夜即降。

translations.literal: The Shizu thereupon with Guang and the others entered the borders of Tangyang at dusk, had each rider carry torches so that they filled the marshes; Guang's flames lit heaven and earth, and the whole city was without exception shaken and terrified, and that night it submitted.
translations.idiomatic: That evening Emperor Guangwu entered Tangyang territory with Ren Guang and the rest; each rider carried a torch until the marshes glittered with fire. The blaze seemed to touch sky and earth, and the whole town panicked and surrendered before dawn.

sentences.zh: 旬日之間，兵眾大盛，因攻城邑，遂屠邯鄲，乃遣光歸郡。

translations.literal: Within ten days the host grew greatly; they thereupon attacked cities and towns, then sacked Handan, and sent Guang back to his commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Within a fortnight their army had swelled; they took towns along the way, stormed Handan, and then sent Ren Guang home to his commandery.

sentences.zh: 城頭子路者，東平人，姓爰，名曾，字子路，與肥城劉詡起兵盧城頭，故號其兵為『城頭子路』。

translations.literal: Chengtou Zilu was a man of Dongping, surname Yuan, given name Zeng, courtesy name Zilu; with Liu Xu of Feicheng he raised troops at Lucheng's wall-head, hence his troops were called "Chengtou Zilu."
translations.idiomatic: Chengtou Zilu was a Dongping man, surname Yuan, personal name Zeng, courtesy Zilu. He and Liu Xu of Feicheng had risen at the ramparts of Lucheng, which is how his band got the nickname Chengtou Zilu.

sentences.zh: 曾自稱『都從事』，詡稱『校三老』，寇掠河、濟間，眾至二十餘萬。

translations.literal: Zeng styled himself "chief clerk"; Xu styled himself "elder of the third rank"; they raided between the He and Ji, and their numbers reached over two hundred thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Zeng called himself chief clerk and Liu Xu called himself elder of the third rank; they plundered the country between the Yellow and Ji rivers until their following topped two hundred thousand.

sentences.zh: 更始立，曾遣使降，拜曾東萊郡太守，詡濟南太守，皆行大將軍事。

translations.literal: When Gengshi was established, Zeng sent envoys to submit; Zeng was appointed governor of Donglai commandery and Xu governor of Jinan, both acting as grand general.
translations.idiomatic: When the Gengshi regime took power, Yuan Zeng sent envoys to surrender and was named governor of Donglai; Liu Xu became governor of Jinan, each with the powers of a grand general.

sentences.zh: 是歲，曾為其將所殺，眾推詡為主，更始封詡助國侯，令罷兵歸本郡。

translations.literal: That year Zeng was killed by his general; the host made Xu their chief; Gengshi enfeoffed Xu as marquis of Zhuguo and ordered him to disband and return to his native commandery.
translations.idiomatic: That same year one of Yuan Zeng's officers murdered him, and the army chose Liu Xu as leader. The Gengshi Emperor enfeoffed Liu Xu as marquis of Zhuguo and told him to demobilize and go home to Jinan.

sentences.zh: 力子都者，東海人也。

translations.literal: Lizi Du was a man of Donghai.
translations.idiomatic: Lizi Du came from Donghai commandery.

sentences.zh: 起兵鄉裏，抄擊徐、兗界，眾有六七萬。

translations.literal: He raised troops in his village and district, raiding the borders of Xu and Yan, and his numbers were sixty or seventy thousand.
translations.idiomatic: He had risen in his home region and raided across Xu and Yan until he commanded sixty or seventy thousand men.

sentences.zh: 更始立，遣使降，拜子都徐州牧。

translations.literal: When Gengshi was established, he sent envoys to submit, and Zidu was appointed shepherd of Xu province.
translations.idiomatic: Under the Gengshi Emperor he too sent envoys to capitulate and was named governor of Xu province.

sentences.zh: 為其部曲所殺，餘黨復相聚，與諸賊會於檀鄉，因號為檀鄉。

translations.literal: He was killed by his personal troops; the remaining faction gathered again, met with various bandits at Tanxiang, and for that reason were called Tanxiang.
translations.idiomatic: His own retainers assassinated him; the survivors regrouped and joined other rebels at Tanxiang, from which the coalition took its name Tanxiang.

sentences.zh: 檀鄉渠帥董次仲始起茌平，遂渡河入魏郡清河，與五校合，眾十餘萬。

translations.literal: The Tanxiang leader Dong Cizhong first rose at Chiping, then crossed the He and entered Wei commandery and Qinghe, joined the Five Camps, and their numbers were over one hundred thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Their chieftain Dong Cizhong had first risen at Chiping, crossed the Yellow River into Wei and Qinghe, merged with the Five Camps band, and commanded well over a hundred thousand fighters.

sentences.zh: 建武元年，世祖入洛陽，遣大司馬吳漢等擊檀鄉，明年春，大破降之。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jianwu the Shizu entered Luoyang and sent Grand Marshal Wu Han and others to strike Tanxiang; the next spring they greatly defeated them and accepted their surrender.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 1 Emperor Guangwu entered Luoyang and dispatched Grand Marshal Wu Han against the Tanxiang rebels; the following spring the imperial army shattered them and took their surrender.

sentences.zh: 是歲，更封光阿陵侯，食邑萬戶。

translations.literal: That year Guang was enfeoffed anew as marquis of Aling with a fief of ten thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: That year Ren Guang was transferred to a new fief as marquis of Aling, with revenue from ten thousand households.

sentences.zh: 五年，征詣京師，奉朝請。

translations.literal: In the fifth year he was summoned to the capital for court audience as fengchaoqing.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year of Jianwu he was called to the capital to attend court as a supernumerary gentleman.

sentences.zh: 其冬卒。

translations.literal: That winter he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died that winter.

sentences.zh: 子隗嗣。

translations.literal: His son Wei succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Ren Wei inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 後阮況為南陽太守，郭唐至河南尹，皆有能名。

translations.literal: Later Ruan Kuang became governor of Nanyang and Guo Tang reached governor of Henan; both had reputations for competence.
translations.idiomatic: Later Ruan Kuang served as governor of Nanyang and Guo Tang rose to governor of Henan; both were remembered as capable officials.

sentences.zh: 隗字仲和，少好黃、老，清靜寡欲，所得奉秩，常以賑恤宗族，收養孤寡。

translations.literal: Wei, courtesy name Zhonghe, in youth loved Huang-Lao, was quiet and had few desires, and what stipend and salary he received he constantly used to relieve his lineage group and to adopt and support orphans and widows.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Wei, styled Zhonghe, had been drawn in youth to Huang-Lao quietism, lived simply, and routinely spent his salary on kinsmen in need and on orphans and widows under his care.

sentences.zh: 顯宗聞之，擢奉朝請，遷羽林左監、虎賁中郎將，又遷長水校尉。

translations.literal: When the Xianzong heard of this, he promoted him to fengchaoqing, moved him to colonel of the left guard of the feathered guard, then general of the household rapid as tiger, then again to colonel of the long river.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ming heard of it and advanced him to supernumerary courtier, then colonel of the left household guard, then general of the rapid-as-tiger household guard, and finally colonel of the Chang River regiment.

sentences.zh: 肅宗即位，雅相敬愛，數稱其行，以為將作大匠。

translations.literal: When the Suzong ascended the throne, he held him in high esteem and love, repeatedly praised his conduct, and appointed him chief architect.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Zhang came to the throne he treated Ren Wei with marked respect, often praised his character, and named him chief architect for the imperial works.

sentences.zh: 將作大匠自建武以來常謁者兼之，至隗乃置真焉。

translations.literal: Since Jianwu the chief architect had constantly been combined with a yeja; only with Wei was a true one appointed.
translations.idiomatic: Since the Jianwu era the chief architect's post had usually been doubled with a court yeja; Ren Wei was the first to hold the office in its own right.

sentences.zh: 建初五年，遷太仆，八年，代竇固為光祿勛，所歷皆有稱。

translations.literal: In the fifth year of Jianchu he was moved to grand coachman; in the eighth year he replaced Dou Gu as superintendent of the Household; in every post he was praised.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 5 he became grand coachman; in Jianchu 8 he succeeded Dou Gu as superintendent of the household, earning praise at every turn.

sentences.zh: 章和元年，拜司空。

translations.literal: In the first year of Zhanghe he was appointed minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhanghe 1 he was named minister of works.

sentences.zh: 隗義行內修，不求名譽，而以沈正見重於世。

translations.literal: Wei's righteous conduct was cultivated inward; he did not seek fame, yet for his depth and uprightness he was held in weight by the age.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Wei cultivated virtue in private without chasing reputation, yet the world esteemed him for his steadiness and integrity.

sentences.zh: 和帝即位，大將軍竇憲秉權，專作威福，內外朝臣莫不震懾。

translations.literal: When the He Emperor ascended the throne, Grand General Dou Xian grasped power, monopolized authority and favor, and inner and outer court ministers were without exception awed.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor He came to the throne, Grand General Dou Xian dominated the government, throwing his weight about until ministers at court and in the provinces quailed before him.

sentences.zh: 時，憲擊匈奴，國用勞費，隗奏議征憲還，前後十上。

translations.literal: At that time Xian was striking the Xiongnu, and state expenses were strained; Wei submitted memorials proposing to recall Xian, ten times in succession.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian was then campaigning against the Xiongnu at crippling cost to the treasury; Ren Wei repeatedly—ten memorials in all—urged the throne to recall him.

sentences.zh: 獨與司徒袁安同心畢力，持重處正，鯁言直議，無所回隱，語在《袁安傳》。

translations.literal: He alone with Minister of Education Yuan An joined hearts and exhausted strength, held steady and stood upright, spoke bluntly and debated directly, without any evasion; the words are in the 《Biography of Yuan An》.
translations.idiomatic: He alone, with Minister of Education Yuan An, held the line with unwavering resolve, spoke plainly without hedging—the full account is in the 《Biography of Yuan An》.

sentences.zh: 永元四年薨，子屯嗣。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Yongyuan he died; his son Tun succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Yongyuan 4; his son Ren Tun inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 帝追思隗忠，擢屯為步兵校尉，徙封西陽侯。

translations.literal: The Emperor recalled Wei's loyalty and promoted Tun to colonel of the infantry guard, and transferred the fief to marquis of Xiyang.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor remembered Ren Wei's loyalty, promoted Ren Tun to colonel of the infantry guard, and moved the enfeoffment to the marquisate of Xiyang.

sentences.zh: 屯卒，子勝嗣。

translations.literal: When Tun died, his son Sheng succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Ren Tun died, his son Ren Sheng succeeded.

sentences.zh: 勝卒，子世嗣，徙封北鄉侯。

translations.literal: When Sheng died, his son Shi succeeded, and the fief was moved to marquis of Beixiang.
translations.idiomatic: When Ren Sheng died, his son Ren Shi inherited, and the fief was shifted to the marquisate of Beixiang.

sentences.zh: 李忠字仲都，東萊黃人也。

translations.literal: Li Zhong, courtesy name Zhongdu, was a native of Huang in Donglai.
translations.idiomatic: Li Zhong, styled Zhongdu, was a native of Huang in Donglai commandery.

sentences.zh: 父為高密都尉。

translations.literal: His father was commandant of Gaomi.
translations.idiomatic: His father had been commandant of Gaomi.

sentences.zh: 忠元始中以父任為郎，署中數十人，而忠獨以好禮修整稱。

translations.literal: In the Yuanshi era Zhong by his father's position became gentleman, and among several dozen attached to the office Zhong alone was praised for loving ritual and being well ordered.
translations.idiomatic: During the Yuanshi reign he entered the corps of gentlemen through his father's rank; of the dozens on staff, he alone was noted for his regard for ritual and orderly conduct.

sentences.zh: 王莽時為新博屬長，郡中鹹敬信之。

translations.literal: In the time of Wang Mang he was chief of Xinbo dependency, and in the commandery all revered and trusted him.
translations.idiomatic: Under Wang Mang he served as chief of the Xinbo dependency office, and the whole commandery respected and trusted him.

sentences.zh: 更始立，使使者行郡國，即拜忠都尉官。

translations.literal: When Gengshi was established, he sent envoys to tour commanderies and kingdoms, and at once appointed Zhong to the office of commandant.
translations.idiomatic: When the Gengshi Emperor took power, his envoys toured the provinces and on the spot commissioned Li Zhong as commandant.

sentences.zh: 忠遂與任光同奉世祖，以為右大將軍，封武固侯。

translations.literal: Zhong thereupon with Ren Guang together served the Shizu, who made him right grand general and enfeoffed him as marquis of Wugu.
translations.idiomatic: Li Zhong then joined Ren Guang in serving Emperor Guangwu, who named him right grand general and marquis of Wugu.

sentences.zh: 時，世祖自解所佩綬以帶忠，因從攻下屬縣。

translations.literal: At that time the Shizu himself untied the sash he wore and gave it to Zhong, and for that reason followed in attacking and taking subordinate counties.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Guangwu unfastened his own official sash and bound it on Li Zhong as a mark of favor, then led him in the reduction of the outlying counties.

sentences.zh: 至苦陘，世祖會諸將，問所得財物，唯忠獨無所掠。

translations.literal: When they reached Kuxing, the Shizu assembled the generals and asked what goods each had taken; only Zhong alone had plundered nothing.
translations.idiomatic: At Kuxing Emperor Guangwu called the generals together and asked what booty each had seized; Li Zhong alone had taken nothing.

sentences.zh: 世祖曰：『我欲特賜，李忠，諸卿得無望乎？

translations.literal: The Shizu said: "I intend to give a special reward to Li Zhong—will you gentlemen perhaps resent it?"
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu said, "I mean to give Li Zhong a special gift—you others won't begrudge him that, I hope?"

sentences.zh: 』即以所乘大驪馬及繡被衣物賜之。

translations.literal: He thereupon bestowed on him the great dapple-gray horse he rode and embroidered coverlets and clothing.
translations.idiomatic: He then gave Li Zhong his own dapple-gray mount, brocade bedding, and fine clothes.

sentences.zh: 進圍巨鹿，未下，王郎遣將攻信都，信都大姓馬寵等開城內之，收太守宗廣及忠母、妻，而令親屬招呼忠。

translations.literal: They advanced to besiege Julu; it had not yet fallen when Wang Lang sent a general to attack Xindu; great clans of Xindu such as Ma Chong opened the city to him within, seized Governor Zong Guang and Zhong's mother and wife, and ordered relatives to summon Zhong.
translations.idiomatic: While the army was still besieging Julu, Wang Lang sent a force against Xindu. Powerful local families led by Ma Chong betrayed the city, seized Governor Zong Guang and Li Zhong's mother and wife, and tried to lure Li Zhong through his kin.

sentences.zh: 時，寵弟從忠為校尉，忠即時召見，責數以背恩反城，因格殺之。

translations.literal: At that time Chong's younger brother followed Zhong as a colonel; Zhong at once summoned him, rebuked him for betraying favor and turning the city, and thereupon struck and killed him.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Chong's brother was serving under Li Zhong as a colonel; Li Zhong called him in immediately, rebuked him for treason in opening the gates, and cut him down on the spot.

sentences.zh: 諸將皆驚曰：『家屬在人手中，殺其弟，何猛也！

translations.literal: The generals all were startled and said: "Your family members are in others' hands—yet you kill his younger brother; how fierce!"
translations.idiomatic: The other generals were stunned: "Your family is hostage to the enemy, and you kill his brother—how ruthless!"

sentences.zh: 』忠曰：『若縱賊不誅，則二心也。

translations.literal: Zhong said: "If I let the traitor go unpunished, that would be a second heart."
translations.idiomatic: Li Zhong replied, "If I spared a traitor, I would be of two minds myself."

sentences.zh: 』世祖聞而美之，謂忠曰：『今吾兵已成矣，將軍可歸救老母、妻、子，宜自募吏民能得家屬者，賜錢千萬，來從我取。

translations.literal: The Shizu heard and praised him, saying to Zhong: "Now my troops are already formed; the general may return to save his old mother, wife, and children—you should yourself recruit officials and people who can recover family members, grant a thousand million cash, and come take it from me."
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu heard and approved: "Our host is now solid. You may go back to rescue your mother, wife, and children—recruit anyone who can recover hostages and I will pay ten million cash; collect it from me."

sentences.zh: 』忠曰：『蒙明公大恩，思得效命，誠不敢內顧宗親。

translations.literal: Zhong said: "Having received the enlightened lord's great grace, I wish only to repay it with my life; I truly dare not look inward to kin."
translations.idiomatic: Li Zhong answered, "I owe you too much to think of anything but repaying it with my life; I cannot spare a thought for my relatives."

sentences.zh: 』世祖乃使任光將兵救信都，光兵於道散降王郎，無功而還。

translations.literal: The Shizu thereupon ordered Ren Guang to lead troops to rescue Xindu; Guang's troops scattered on the road and surrendered to Wang Lang, and returned without achievement.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Guangwu then sent Ren Guang to relieve Xindu, but Ren Guang's men melted away on the march and defected to Wang Lang, so the expedition accomplished nothing.

sentences.zh: 會更始遣將攻破信都，忠家屬得全。

translations.literal: It happened that Gengshi sent a general who broke Xindu, and Zhong's family members were preserved whole.
translations.idiomatic: Soon afterward a Gengshi general retook Xindu, and Li Zhong's family came through unharmed.

sentences.zh: 世祖因使忠還，行太守事，收郡中大姓附邯鄲者，誅殺數百人。

translations.literal: The Shizu thereupon ordered Zhong to return and act as governor, arresting great clans of the commandery who had sided with Handan, and executed several hundred.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu then sent Li Zhong back as acting governor to round up leading families who had backed Wang Lang of Handan; several hundred were executed.

sentences.zh: 及任光歸郡，忠乃還復為都尉。

translations.literal: When Ren Guang returned to his commandery, Zhong then returned again to be commandant.
translations.idiomatic: After Ren Guang went home to his commandery, Li Zhong resumed his post as commandant.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，更封中水侯，食邑三千戶。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu he was enfeoffed anew as marquis of Zhongshui with a fief of three thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 he was re-enfeoffed as marquis of Zhongshui with income from three thousand households.

sentences.zh: 其年，征拜五官中郎將，從平寵萌，董憲等。

translations.literal: That year he was summoned and appointed general of the household for the five offices, and followed in pacifying Pang Meng, Dong Xian, and others.
translations.idiomatic: The same year he was called to court as general of the household for the five offices and took part in the campaigns against Pang Meng and Dong Xian.

sentences.zh: 六年，遷丹陽太守。

translations.literal: In the sixth year he was moved to governor of Danyang.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 6 he became governor of Danyang.

sentences.zh: 是時，海內新定，南方海濱江淮，多擁兵據土。

translations.literal: At that time within the seas had newly been settled, but south of the sea along the rivers and Huai many held troops and occupied territory.
translations.idiomatic: The empire had only just been pacified, yet along the southern coast and the Yangzi-Huai region many still held arms and carved out their own ground.

sentences.zh: 忠到郡，招懷降附，其不服者悉誅之，旬月皆平。

translations.literal: When Zhong reached the commandery, he summoned and cherished those who submitted; those who would not submit he utterly executed, and within a month all was pacified.
translations.idiomatic: Li Zhong won over those willing to yield and exterminated those who refused; within a month the commandery was quiet.

sentences.zh: 忠以丹陽越俗不好學，嫁娶禮儀，衰於中國，乃為起學校，習禮容，春秋鄉飲，選用明經，郡中向慕之。

translations.literal: Zhong because the Yue customs of Danyang did not love learning, and marriage rites had declined compared with the central states, therefore for them established schools, practiced ritual deportment, held spring and autumn village drinking, selected men learned in the classics, and the commandery turned toward and admired this.
translations.idiomatic: Finding the local Yue people little inclined toward scholarship and their wedding etiquette lax compared with the heartland, he founded schools, taught ritual bearing, held the classic village-drinking ceremony, and promoted men versed in the classics until the commandery took notice and began to emulate them.

sentences.zh: 墾田增多，三歲間流民占著者五萬餘口。

translations.literal: Reclaimed fields increased, and within three years wandering people who settled numbered over fifty thousand mouths.
translations.idiomatic: Under his administration newly opened fields multiplied, and in three years more than fifty thousand displaced people had registered as residents.

sentences.zh: 十四年，三公奏課為天下第一，遷豫章太守。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year the three dukes reported his assessment as first in the realm, and he was moved to governor of Yuzhang.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 14 the three highest ministers ranked his performance first in the empire, and he was transferred to Yuzhang.

sentences.zh: 病去官，征詣京師。

translations.literal: Ill he left office and was summoned to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Illness forced him to resign; he was then summoned to the capital.

sentences.zh: 十九年，卒。

translations.literal: In the nineteenth year he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Jianwu 19.

sentences.zh: 子威嗣。

translations.literal: His son Wei succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Li Wei inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 威卒，子純嗣，永平九年，坐母殺純叔父。

translations.literal: When Wei died, his son Chun succeeded; in the ninth year of Yongping, because his mother killed Chun's paternal uncle.
translations.idiomatic: When Li Wei died, his son Li Chun succeeded; in Yongping 9 the title ran into trouble because Li Chun's mother had murdered his father's younger brother.

sentences.zh: 國除。

translations.literal: The state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: The marquisate was struck off.

sentences.zh: 永初七年，鄧太后復封純琴亭侯。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of Yongchu Empress Dowager Deng again enfeoffed Chun as marquis of Qinting.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 7 Empress Dowager Deng restored Li Chun as marquis of Qinting.

sentences.zh: 純卒，子廣嗣。

translations.literal: When Chun died, his son Guang succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Li Chun died, his son Li Guang inherited.

sentences.zh: 萬脩字君遊，扶風茂陵人也。

translations.literal: Wan Xiu, courtesy name Junyou, was a native of Maoling in Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: Wan Xiu, styled Junyou, came from Maoling in Fufeng commandery.

sentences.zh: 更始時，為信都令，與太守任光、都尉李忠共城守，迎世祖，拜為偏將軍，封造義侯。

translations.literal: In the time of Gengshi he was magistrate of Xindu; with Governor Ren Guang and Commandant Li Zhong he together defended the city walls, welcomed the Shizu, was appointed lieutenant general, and enfeoffed as marquis of Zaoyi.
translations.idiomatic: Under Gengshi he was magistrate of Xindu; with Governor Ren Guang and Commandant Li Zhong he held the city, welcomed Emperor Guangwu, was named lieutenant general, and enfeoffed as marquis of Zaoyi.

sentences.zh: 及破邯鄲，拜右將軍，從平河北。

translations.literal: When Handan was broken, he was appointed right general and followed in pacifying Hebei.
translations.idiomatic: After the fall of Handan he became right general and helped pacify Hebei.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，更封槐裏侯。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu he was enfeoffed anew as marquis of Huaili.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 his fief was moved to Huaili.

sentences.zh: 與揚化將軍堅鐔俱擊南陽，未克而病，卒於軍。

translations.literal: Together with General Who Spreads Transformation Jian Tan he struck Nanyang; before it was taken he fell ill and died in the army.
translations.idiomatic: He campaigned with General Who Spreads Transformation Jian Tan against Nanyang but fell ill before the city fell and died in camp.

sentences.zh: 子普嗣，徙封泫氏侯。

translations.literal: His son Pu succeeded, and the fief was moved to marquis of Xuanshi.
translations.idiomatic: His son Wan Pu inherited, and the title was shifted to the marquisate of Xuanshi.

sentences.zh: 普卒，子親嗣，徙封扶柳侯。

translations.literal: When Pu died, his son Qin succeeded, and the fief was moved to marquis of Filiu.
translations.idiomatic: When Wan Pu died, his son Wan Qin succeeded, and the fief became Filiu.

sentences.zh: 親卒，無子，國除。

translations.literal: When Qin died, there was no son, and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Wan Qin left no heir, so the marquisate lapsed.

sentences.zh: 永初七年，鄧太后紹封脩曾孫豐為曲平亭侯。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of Yongchu Empress Dowager Deng continued the line and enfeoffed Xiu's great-grandson Feng as marquis of Qupingting.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 7 Empress Dowager Deng renewed the line, enfeoffing Wan Xiu's great-grandson Wan Feng as marquis of Qupingting.

sentences.zh: 豐卒，子熾嗣。

translations.literal: When Feng died, his son Chi succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Wan Feng died, his son Wan Chi inherited.

sentences.zh: 永建元年}，熾卒，無子，國除。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongjian, Chi died; there was no son, and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongjian 1 Wan Chi died without issue and the fief ended.

sentences.zh: 延熹二年，桓帝紹封脩玄孫恭為門德亭侯。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yanxi Emperor Huan continued the line and enfeoffed Xiu's great-great-grandson Gong as marquis of Mendeting.
translations.idiomatic: In Yanxi 2 Emperor Huan revived the line, naming Wan Xiu's descendant Wan Gong marquis of Mendeting.

sentences.zh: 邳彤字偉君，信都人也。

translations.literal: Pi Tong, courtesy name Weijun, was a native of Xindu.
translations.idiomatic: Pi Tong, styled Weijun, was a man of Xindu.

sentences.zh: 父吉，為遼西太守。

translations.literal: His father Ji had been governor of Liaoxi.
translations.idiomatic: His father Pi Ji had served as governor of Liaoxi.

sentences.zh: 彤初為王莽和成卒正。

translations.literal: Tong at first was zuzheng of Hegcheng under Wang Mang.
translations.idiomatic: Pi Tong began his career as zuzheng of Hegcheng under Wang Mang.

sentences.zh: 世祖徇河北，至下曲陽，彤舉城降，復以為太守，留止數日。

translations.literal: When the Shizu campaigned through Hebei and reached Xiaquyang, Tong raised the city in surrender; he was again appointed governor and detained several days.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Guangwu advanced through Hebei and reached Xiaquyang, Pi Tong surrendered the city; Guangwu reappointed him governor and kept him there for a few days.

sentences.zh: 世祖北至薊，會王郎兵起，使其將徇地，所到縣莫不奉迎，唯和成、信都堅守不下。

translations.literal: When the Shizu went north to Ji, it happened that Wang Lang's troops rose; he sent his generals to seize territory, and in every county they reached none failed to welcome them—only Hegcheng and Xindu held fast and would not yield.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu then rode north to Ji just as Wang Lang's rebellion erupted. Wang's officers swept the countryside, and county after county opened its gates—only Hegcheng and Xindu refused to submit.

sentences.zh: 彤聞世祖從薊還，失軍，欲至信都，乃先使五官掾張萬、督郵尹綏，選精騎二千餘匹，緣路迎世祖軍。

translations.literal: Tong heard the Shizu was returning from Ji having lost his army and intending to reach Xindu; he first sent registrar Zhang Wan and touring investigator Yin Sui to select more than two thousand picked horsemen to meet the Shizu's army along the road.
translations.idiomatic: Hearing that Guangwu was retreating from Ji stripped of his main force and heading for Xindu, Pi Tong sent registrar Zhang Wan and touring investigator Yin Sui ahead with over two thousand picked cavalry to escort him along the route.

sentences.zh: 彤尋與世祖會信都。

translations.literal: Tong soon thereafter joined the Shizu at Xindu.
translations.idiomatic: Pi Tong soon joined Emperor Guangwu at Xindu.

sentences.zh: 世祖雖得二郡之助，而兵眾未合，議者多言可因信都兵自送，西還長安。

translations.literal: Although the Shizu had obtained the aid of the two commanderies, his host was not yet united, and those who debated mostly said he could ride Xindu's troops to escort himself west back to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Even with two loyal commanderies behind him, Guangwu's army was still scattered; many advisers urged him to use Xindu's men as an escort and withdraw west to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 彤廷對曰：

translations.literal: Tong replied in court:
translations.idiomatic: Pi Tong rose in council and said:

sentences.zh: 議者之言皆非也。

translations.literal: The words of those who debate are all wrong.
translations.idiomatic: Those counsels are mistaken.

sentences.zh: 吏民歌吟思漢久矣，故更始舉尊號而天下響應，三輔清宮除道以迎之。

translations.literal: Officials and people have long sung and sighed, longing for Han; therefore when Gengshi raised the exalted title the realm answered like an echo, and the three adjuncts cleared the palaces and swept the roads to welcome him.
translations.idiomatic: Officials and commoners have longed for the house of Han; when the Gengshi Emperor claimed the throne, the empire rallied, and the capital region swept the avenues to receive him.

sentences.zh: 一夫荷戟大呼，則千里之將無不捐城遁逃，虜伏請降。

translations.literal: If one man shouldered a halberd and shouted aloud, then generals for a thousand li would without exception abandon cities and flee in hiding, or crouch in submission begging to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: A single man with a halberd, raising his voice, could send commanders a thousand li away abandoning their walls or kneeling to yield.

sentences.zh: 自上古以來，亦未有感物動民其如此者也。

translations.literal: From high antiquity onward, there has never been one who moved things and stirred the people like this.
translations.idiomatic: Never in recorded memory had popular feeling swung so completely behind a cause.

sentences.zh: 又卜者王郎，假名因勢，驅集烏合之眾，遂震燕、趙之地；

translations.literal: Moreover the diviner Wang Lang, borrowing a name and riding the moment, drove together a flock of crows and sparrows, and thereupon shook the lands of Yan and Zhao;
translations.idiomatic: Wang Lang the fortune-teller had seized power with a borrowed name and a rabble, yet he still overawed Yan and Zhao.

sentences.zh: 況明公奮二郡之兵，揚響應之威，以攻則何城不克，以戰則何軍不服！

translations.literal: How much more, when the enlightened lord rouses the troops of two commanderies and raises the might of answering echoes—with such a force what city would not be taken, with such an army what host would not submit!
translations.idiomatic: How much more may you, my lord, rouse two loyal commanderies and the prestige of a nation that still answers Han—what wall could stand against you, what army refuse you!

sentences.zh: 今釋此而歸，豈徒空失河北，必要驚動三輔，墮損威重，非計之得者也。

translations.literal: Now to abandon this and return—would it not only empty-handed lose Hebei, but necessarily also startle the three adjuncts, topple and injure awe-inspiring weight; it is not a plan that gains.
translations.idiomatic: To throw this away and march west would not only forfeit Hebei for nothing; it would shake the heartland and tarnish your authority—no winning strategy.

sentences.zh: 若明公無復征伐之意，則雖信都之兵猶難會也。

translations.literal: If the enlightened lord has no further mind to campaign, then even Xindu's troops would still be hard to assemble.
translations.idiomatic: If you show the world you will not fight on, even the men of Xindu may not rally again.

sentences.zh: 何者？

translations.literal: Why?
translations.idiomatic: Why is that?

sentences.zh: 明公既西，則邯鄲城民不肯捐父母、背城主，而千里送公，其離散亡逃可必也。

translations.literal: When the enlightened lord has gone west, then the people of Handan city will not be willing to abandon parents and betray their city lord, and to escort the lord a thousand li; their scattering, fleeing, and running away can be certain.
translations.idiomatic: Once you turn west, the people of Handan will not leave parents and lords to follow you a thousand li; your following would melt away for certain.

sentences.zh: 世祖善其言而止。

translations.literal: The Shizu thought his words good and stopped.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Guangwu judged the argument sound and abandoned the plan to withdraw.

sentences.zh: 即日拜彤為後大將軍，和成太守如故，使將兵居前。

translations.literal: That day he appointed Tong rear grand general, his post as governor of Hegcheng remaining as before, and ordered him to lead troops in the van.
translations.idiomatic: That same day he named Pi Tong rear grand general while leaving him governor of Hegcheng, and put his command in the lead.

sentences.zh: 比至堂陽，堂陽已反屬王郎，彤使張萬、尹綏先曉譬吏民，世祖夜至，即開門出迎。

translations.literal: By the time they reached Tangyang, Tangyang had already turned back to Wang Lang; Tong sent Zhang Wan and Yin Sui first to explain clearly to officials and people; the Shizu arrived by night, and at once they opened the gates and came out to welcome.
translations.idiomatic: By the time they reached Tangyang, the town had gone over to Wang Lang. Pi Tong sent Zhang Wan and Yin Sui to reason with the people; Guangwu arrived after dark, and the gates flew open in welcome.

sentences.zh: 引兵擊破白奢賊於中山。

translations.literal: He led troops and broke the bandits of Baisha in Zhongshan.
translations.idiomatic: He then led the column that routed the Baisha bandits in Zhongshan.

sentences.zh: 自此常從戰攻。

translations.literal: From this he constantly followed in battle and attack.
translations.idiomatic: After that he fought at Guangwu's side in every major action.

sentences.zh: 信都復反為王郎，郎所置信都王捕系彤父弟及妻子，使為手書呼彤曰：『降者封爵，不降族滅。

translations.literal: Xindu again rebelled to Wang Lang; the Prince of Xindu whom Lang had installed seized Tong's father, younger brothers, wife, and children, and ordered him to write in his own hand summoning Tong, saying: "Those who submit will be enfeoffed with rank; those who do not submit will be exterminated to the lineage."
translations.idiomatic: Xindu rebelled again to Wang Lang. Wang's puppet Prince of Xindu seized Pi Tong's father, brothers, wife, and children and forced him to write: "Surrender and win a title; resist and your clan dies."

sentences.zh: 』彤涕泣報曰：『事君者不得顧家。

translations.literal: Tong weeping replied: "One who serves a lord cannot attend to family."
translations.idiomatic: Pi Tong answered through tears, "A man who serves his prince cannot think first of his kin."

sentences.zh: 彤親屬所以至今得安於信都者，劉公之恩也。

translations.literal: The reason Tong's kin until now have been able to dwell in peace at Xindu is the grace of Lord Liu.
translations.idiomatic: My family has stayed safe in Xindu only because of Lord Liu's protection."

sentences.zh: 公方爭國事，彤不得復念私也。

translations.literal: The lord is now contending for the state affair; Tong cannot again think of private matters."
translations.idiomatic: You are fighting for the realm; I cannot weigh private ties."

sentences.zh: 』會更始所遣將攻拔信都，郎兵敗走，彤家屬得免。

translations.literal: It happened that the general Gengshi sent attacked and took Xindu; Lang's troops were defeated and fled, and Tong's family members obtained escape.
translations.idiomatic: Soon an imperial general sent by the Gengshi court recaptured Xindu, Wang Lang's men fled, and Pi Tong's family went free.

sentences.zh: 彤卒，子湯嗣，九年，徙封樂陵侯。

translations.literal: When Tong died, his son Tang succeeded; in the ninth year the fief was moved to marquis of Leling.
translations.idiomatic: When Pi Tong died, his son Pi Tang inherited; in the ninth year of Jianwu the title was transferred to the marquisate of Leling.

sentences.zh: 十九年，湯卒，子某嗣；

translations.literal: In the nineteenth year Tang died, and a son succeeded;
translations.idiomatic: Pi Tang died in Jianwu 19; a son inherited, though the annals omit his personal name.

sentences.zh: 無子，國除。

translations.literal: There was no son, and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: That son left no heir, and the marquisate lapsed.

sentences.zh: 元初元年，鄧太后紹封彤孫音為平亭侯。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yuanchu Empress Dowager Deng continued the line and enfeoffed Tong's grandson Yin as marquis of Pingting.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanchu 1 Empress Dowager Deng restored the line, enfeoffing Pi Tong's grandson Pi Yin as marquis of Pingting.

sentences.zh: 音卒、子柴嗣。

translations.literal: When Yin died, his son Chai succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Pi Yin died, his son Pi Chai inherited.

sentences.zh: 初，張萬，尹綏與彤俱迎世祖，皆拜偏將軍，亦從征伐。

translations.literal: At first Zhang Wan and Yin Sui together with Tong welcomed the Shizu; all were appointed lieutenant generals and also followed in campaigns.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Wan and Yin Sui had ridden out with Pi Tong to receive Guangwu; each was named lieutenant general and saw action in the wars.

sentences.zh: 萬封重平侯，綏封平臺侯。

translations.literal: Wan was enfeoffed as marquis of Chongping, Sui as marquis of Pingtai.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Wan became marquis of Chongping and Yin Sui marquis of Pingtai.

sentences.zh: 論曰：凡言成事者，以功著易顯；

translations.literal: The judgment says: In general those who speak of completed affairs—because achievement is manifest, they are easy to display;
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: finished deeds shine bright because their merit is visible;

sentences.zh: 謀幾初者，以理隱難昭。

translations.literal: those who plan at the first hint—because principle is hidden, they are hard to make clear.
translations.idiomatic: counsel offered at the turning point stays obscure because its rightness is harder to see.

sentences.zh: 斯固原情比跡，所宜推察者也。

translations.literal: This is indeed what, tracing feeling and comparing traces, one ought to push and examine.
translations.idiomatic: Both deserve careful weighing against the evidence of events.

sentences.zh: 若乃議者欲因二郡之眾，建入關之策，委成業，臨不測，而世主未悟，謀夫景同，邳彤之廷對，其為幾乎！

translations.literal: As for those debaters who wished to rely on the masses of two commanderies to build a plan of entering the Pass, to cast away completed enterprise and face the unfathomable, while the age's lord had not yet awakened and planners shared one view—Tong's reply in court, was it not close to the mark!
translations.idiomatic: Consider the advisers who would have marched two loyal commanderies west through the Pass, abandoning a winning position for an unknown—while their lord still hesitated. Pi Tong's speech in council was perilously close to the truth of the moment!

sentences.zh: 語曰『一言可以興邦』，斯近之矣。

translations.literal: The saying goes, "One word can raise a state"; this is near to it.
translations.idiomatic: The proverb says one sentence can restore a kingdom; this comes close.

sentences.zh: 劉植字伯先，巨鹿昌城人也。

translations.literal: Liu Zhi, courtesy name Boxian, was a native of Changcheng in Julu.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Zhi, styled Boxian, came from Changcheng in Julu commandery.

sentences.zh: 王郎起，植與弟喜、從兄歆率宗族賓客，聚兵數千人據昌城。

translations.literal: When Wang Lang rose, Zhi with his younger brother Xi and elder cousin Xin led lineage and retainers, gathered several thousand troops, and occupied Changcheng.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Lang rebelled, Liu Zhi, his brother Liu Xi, and cousin Liu Xin rallied clan and clients, several thousand strong, and held Changcheng.

sentences.zh: 聞世祖從薊還，乃開門迎世祖，以植為驍騎將軍，喜、歆偏將軍，皆為列侯。

translations.literal: Hearing the Shizu was returning from Ji, they thereupon opened the gates to welcome the Shizu; Zhi was appointed general of nimble cavalry, Xi and Xin lieutenant generals; all became full marquises.
translations.idiomatic: Learning that Guangwu was marching back from Ji, they opened the gates to him. Liu Zhi became general of nimble cavalry, Liu Xi and Liu Xin lieutenant generals, each enfeoffed at full marquis rank.

sentences.zh: 時真定王劉揚起兵以附王郎，眾十餘萬，世祖遣植說揚，揚乃降。

translations.literal: At that time the Prince of Zhending Liu Yang raised troops to join Wang Lang, his numbers over one hundred thousand; the Shizu sent Zhi to persuade Yang, and Yang thereupon submitted.
translations.idiomatic: The Prince of Zhending, Liu Yang, had thrown in with Wang Lang at the head of more than a hundred thousand men; Guangwu sent Liu Zhi to parley, and Liu Yang yielded.

sentences.zh: 世祖因留真定，納郭后，後即揚之甥也，故以此結之。

translations.literal: The Shizu thereupon stayed at Zhending and took Empress Guo, who afterward was Yang's niece; for that reason he bound him with this.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu lingered at Zhending and married the future Empress Guo—Liu Yang's niece—thereby cementing the alliance.

sentences.zh: 乃與揚及諸將置酒郭氏漆裏舍，揚擊築為歡，因得進兵拔邯鄲，從平河北。

translations.literal: Thereupon with Yang and the various generals he set wine at the Guo residence in Qili; Yang beat the zhu for joy, and for that reason was able to advance troops and take Handan, and followed in pacifying Hebei.
translations.idiomatic: He feasted Liu Yang and his generals at the Guo family house in Qili; Liu Yang struck the chime-sticks in merriment, then joined the advance that stormed Handan and finished the pacification of Hebei.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，更封植為昌城侯。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianwu Zhi was enfeoffed anew as marquis of Changcheng.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 Liu Zhi was re-enfeoffed as marquis of Changcheng.

sentences.zh: 討密縣賊，戰歿。

translations.literal: He attacked the bandits of Mi county and died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: He fell leading the attack on the rebels at Mi county.

sentences.zh: 子向嗣。

translations.literal: His son Xiang succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Liu Xiang inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 帝使喜代將植營，復為驍騎將軍，封觀津侯。

translations.literal: The Emperor ordered Xi to replace command of Zhi's camp, again as general of nimble cavalry, enfeoffed as marquis of Guanjin.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor put Liu Xi in command of his brother's troops as general of nimble cavalry and enfeoffed him as marquis of Guanjin.

sentences.zh: 喜卒，復以歆為驍騎將軍，封浮陽侯。

translations.literal: When Xi died, Xin was again made general of nimble cavalry, enfeoffed as marquis of Fuyang.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Xi died, Liu Xin took the same post and became marquis of Fuyang.

sentences.zh: 喜、歆從征伐，皆傳國於後。

translations.literal: Xi and Xin followed in campaigns; both transmitted the state to posterity.
translations.idiomatic: Both brothers campaigned through the wars, and their lines kept the marquisates for generations.

sentences.zh: 向徙封東武陽侯，卒，子述嗣，永平十五年，坐與楚王英謀反，國除。

translations.literal: Xiang was moved in fief to marquis of Dongwuyang; when he died, his son Shu succeeded; in the fifteenth year of Yongping, because he sat in conspiracy with the Prince of Chu Ying to rebel, the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang's fief shifted to Dongwuyang; his son Liu Shu succeeded but lost the title in Yongping 15 for plotting with Prince Ying of Chu.

sentences.zh: 耿純字伯山，巨鹿宋子人也。

translations.literal: Geng Chun, courtesy name Boshan, was a native of Songzi in Julu.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun, styled Boshan, was a man of Songzi in Julu.

sentences.zh: 父艾，為王莽濟平尹。

translations.literal: His father Ai had been Jiping yin under Wang Mang.
translations.idiomatic: His father Geng Ai had served Wang Mang as Jiping intendant.

sentences.zh: 純學於長安，因除為納言士。

translations.literal: Chun studied in Chang'an and for that reason was appointed gentleman of the reception of words.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun studied at the capital and received appointment as a reception gentleman under Wang Mang.

sentences.zh: 王莽敗，更始立，使舞陰王李軼降諸郡國，純父艾降，還為濟南太守。

translations.literal: When Wang Mang fell and Gengshi was established, he sent the Prince of Wuyin Li Yi to subdue the commanderies and kingdoms; Chun's father Ai submitted and returned as governor of Jinan.
translations.idiomatic: After Wang Mang fell and the Gengshi Emperor took power, Prince Li Yi of Wuyin toured the provinces to accept surrenders; Geng Ai submitted and was sent back as governor of Jinan.

sentences.zh: 時李軼兄弟用事，專制方面，賓客遊說者甚眾。

translations.literal: At that time Li Yi's brothers held power and monopolized their quarter; retainers who wandered persuading were very numerous.
translations.idiomatic: The Li brothers then dominated their region, and a crowd of hangers-on importuned them daily.

sentences.zh: 純連求謁不得通，久之乃得見，因說軼曰：『大王以龍虎之姿，遭風雲之時，奮迅拔起，期月之間兄弟稱王，而德信不聞於士民，功勞未施於百姓，寵祿暴興，此智者之所忌也。

translations.literal: Chun repeatedly sought audience and could not get through; after a long time he at last obtained a meeting, and thereupon said to Yi: "The great king, with the bearing of dragon and tiger, encountered the time of wind and cloud; he rose swift and tall, and within a month between brothers called themselves kings—yet virtue and trust are not heard among knights and people, merit and labor are not yet applied to the hundred surnames, favor and salary have burst forth in rise; this is what the wise fear."
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun could not win an audience for a long time; when he finally saw Li Yi he said, "You have the stature of dragon and tiger and caught the moment of storm; your house declared itself kings within a month—yet the people hear nothing of your good faith, see no real benefit, only sudden honors. The wise fear exactly that."

sentences.zh: 兢兢自危，猶懼不終，而況沛然自足，可以成功者乎？

translations.literal: "Trembling in self-danger, still fearing not to reach the end—how much more when overflowing and self-sufficient, can one thereby succeed?"
translations.idiomatic: "Even men who tremble for their lives fear they will not last—how can the smug hope to finish what they began?"

sentences.zh: 』軼奇之，且以其巨鹿大姓，乃承制拜為騎都尉，授以節，令安集趙、魏。

translations.literal: Yi thought him extraordinary, and moreover because he was a great surname of Julu, thereupon on his own authority appointed him colonel of cavalry, gave him credentials, and ordered him to pacify Zhao and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Li Yi was impressed, and because the Gengs were a powerful Julu clan, he commissioned Geng Chun colonel of cavalry on his own authority, handed him the staff of office, and sent him to pacify Zhao and Wei.

sentences.zh: 會世祖度河至邯鄲，純即謁見，世祖深接之。

translations.literal: It happened the Shizu crossed the He and reached Handan; Chun at once had audience, and the Shizu deeply received him.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Guangwu crossed the Yellow River to Handan, Geng Chun presented himself and was welcomed with unusual warmth.

sentences.zh: 純退，見官屬將兵法度不與它將同，遂求自結納，獻馬及縑帛數百匹。

translations.literal: Chun withdrew, saw that the discipline of officials and generals was not the same as other generals, and thereupon sought to bind himself in friendship, presenting horses and several hundred bolts of silk.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun withdrew, noticed that Guangwu's staff ran tighter than other commands, and returned with horses and hundreds of bolts of silk to cement his allegiance.

sentences.zh: 世祖北至中山，留純邯鄲。

translations.literal: The Shizu went north to Zhongshan and left Chun at Handan.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu marched north into Zhongshan, he left Geng Chun behind at Handan.

sentences.zh: 會王郎反，世祖自薊東南馳，純與從昆弟訢、宿、植共率宗族賓客二千餘人，老病者皆載木自隨，奉迎於育。

translations.literal: It happened Wang Lang rebelled; the Shizu galloped southeast from Ji; Chun with cousins Xin, Su, and Zhi together led lineage and retainers over two thousand persons, the old and sick all carrying wood with them, and welcomed him at Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Lang's revolt broke out while Guangwu was racing southeast from Ji; Geng Chun and his cousins Geng Xin, Geng Su, and Geng Zhi brought more than two thousand kinsmen and clients—even the elderly carried firewood as provisions—to meet him at Yu.

sentences.zh: 拜純為前將軍，封耿鄉侯，訢、宿、植皆偏將軍，使與純居前，降宋子，從攻下曲陽及中山。

translations.literal: Chun was appointed forward general, enfeoffed as marquis of Gengxiang; Xin, Su, and Zhi were all lieutenant generals; he ordered them with Chun to go in front, submit Songzi, and follow in attacking Xiaquyang and Zhongshan.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun became forward general and marquis of Gengxiang; Geng Xin, Geng Su, and Geng Zhi were named lieutenant generals and sent ahead with him to win over Songzi and then strike Xiaquyang and Zhongshan.

sentences.zh: 是時，郡國多降邯鄲者，純恐宗家懷異心，乃使訢、宿歸燒其廬舍。

translations.literal: At that time many commanderies and kingdoms had submitted to Handan; Chun feared his clan might harbor a different heart, and thereupon sent Xin and Su back to burn their houses.
translations.idiomatic: Many districts had gone over to Wang Lang; fearing divided loyalties in his own clan, Geng Chun sent Geng Xin and Geng Su home to burn the family compounds.

sentences.zh: 世祖問純故，對曰：『竊見明公單車臨河北，非有府臧之蓄，重賞甘餌，可以聚人者也，徒以恩德懷之，是故士眾樂附。

translations.literal: The Shizu asked Chun the reason; he replied: "Privately I saw the enlightened lord with a single cart came to Hebei, not having stored wealth in treasuries, nor heavy bribes and sweet bait by which to gather people; he relied solely on grace and virtue to cherish them, and for that reason knights and masses joyfully attached."
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu asked why. Geng Chun answered, "You came north almost alone, with no treasury of gold to buy men—only your virtue drew them. That is why they follow."

sentences.zh: 今邯鄲自立，北州疑惑，純雖舉族歸命，老弱在行，猶恐宗人賓客半有不同心者，故燔燒屋室，絕其反顧之望。

translations.literal: "Now Handan has set itself up, and the northern provinces hesitate; although Chun has raised the whole clan to submit to fate, old and weak are in the ranks, still I fear that among lineage and retainers half may have hearts not of one mind; therefore I burned houses and cut off their hope of turning back."
translations.idiomatic: Handan still claims the throne and the north wavers. I brought every Geng, young and old, to your banner, yet I feared half our retainers might still look homeward—so I burned the roofs behind them."

sentences.zh: 』世祖漢息。

translations.literal: The Shizu sighed.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Guangwu drew a long breath at that.

sentences.zh: 及至鄗，世祖止傳舍，鄗大姓蘇公反城開門內王郎將李惲。

translations.literal: When they reached Hao, the Shizu stopped at the relay inn; the great surname Su of Hao turned the city in revolt, opened the gates, and admitted Wang Lang's general Li Yun.
translations.idiomatic: At Hao, Guangwu halted at the post station. A local magnate named Su betrayed the town to Wang Lang's officer Li Yun.

sentences.zh: 純先覺知，將兵逆與惲戰，大破斬之。

translations.literal: Chun first perceived it, led troops to meet Yun in battle, greatly defeated him, and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun sensed the plot first, sortied against Li Yun, shattered his force, and took his head.

sentences.zh: 從平邯鄲，又破銅馬。

translations.literal: He followed in pacifying Handan and again broke the Bronze Horse.
translations.idiomatic: He went on to help reduce Handan and crush the Bronze Horse band.

sentences.zh: 時，赤眉、青犢、上江、大彤、鐵脛、五幡十餘萬眾並在射犬，世祖引兵將擊之。

translations.literal: At that time the Red Eyebrows, Green Calves, Upper Yang, Great Tong, Iron Shin, and Five Banners—over one hundred thousand together—were all at Shequan; the Shizu led troops about to strike them.
translations.idiomatic: More than a hundred thousand men of the Red Eyebrows, Green Calves, Upper Yang, Great Tong, Iron Shanks, and Five Banners bands were massed at Shequan; Guangwu advanced to attack them.

sentences.zh: 純軍在前，去眾營數裏，賊忽夜攻純，雨射營中，士多死傷。

translations.literal: Chun's army was in front, several li from the mass camp; the bandits suddenly by night attacked Chun, raining arrows into the camp, and many soldiers died or were wounded.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun's division camped a few li from the enemy; the rebels rushed his lines by night, arrow volleys riddled the tents, and casualties mounted.

sentences.zh: 純勒部曲，堅守不動。

translations.literal: Chun ordered his personal troops, firmly held and did not move.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun held his men steady and refused to buckle.

sentences.zh: 選敢死二千人，俱持強弩，各傅三矢，使銜枚間行，繞出賊後，齊聲呼噪，強弩並發，賊眾驚走，追擊，遂破之。

translations.literal: He selected two thousand dare-to-die, all bearing strong crossbows, each with three bolts fitted, ordered them to hold rods in the teeth and move by hidden paths, circle behind the bandits, together shout and clamor, strong crossbows all discharged at once; the bandit mass was startled and fled; pursuing the attack, he thereupon broke them.
translations.idiomatic: He chose two thousand volunteers, each with a heavy crossbow and three bolts, had them advance in silence with bits between their teeth, swing behind the rebel camp, then rise shouting; a single volley broke the enemy, and a pursuit finished them.

sentences.zh: 馳騎白世祖。

translations.literal: He galloped riders to report white to the Shizu.
translations.idiomatic: He sent horsemen galloping to report to Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 世祖明旦與諸將俱至營，勞純曰：『昨夜困乎？

translations.literal: The Shizu next morning with the various generals all came to the camp and consoled Chun, saying: "Were you hard pressed last night?"
translations.idiomatic: At dawn Guangwu rode up with his generals to praise Geng Chun: "A rough night?"

sentences.zh: 』純曰：『賴明公威德，幸而獲全。

translations.literal: Chun said: "Relying on the enlightened lord's awesome virtue, fortunately I obtained preservation whole."
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun answered, "Your majesty's prestige saw us through."

sentences.zh: 』世祖曰：『大兵不可夜動，故不相救耳。

translations.literal: The Shizu said: "Great troops cannot move by night; therefore I did not rescue each other."
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu said, "A large army cannot march by night—that is why I could not relieve you sooner."

sentences.zh: 軍營進退無常，卿宗族不可悉居軍中。

translations.literal: Army camps advance and retreat without constancy; your lineage group cannot all dwell in the army."
translations.idiomatic: Camps shift too often for your whole clan to stay inside the lines."

sentences.zh: 』乃以純族人耿伋為蒲吾長，悉令將親屬居焉。

translations.literal: Thereupon he appointed Chun's clansman Geng Ji as magistrate of Puwu and ordered all to lead relatives to dwell there.
translations.idiomatic: He named Geng Chun's kinsman Geng Ji magistrate of Puwu and sent the family dependents to live there under his care.

sentences.zh: 世祖即位，封純高陽侯。

translations.literal: When the Shizu ascended the throne, he enfeoffed Chun as marquis of Gaoyang.
translations.idiomatic: At Guangwu's accession Geng Chun became marquis of Gaoyang.

sentences.zh: 擊劉永於濟陰，下定陶。

translations.literal: He struck Liu Yong at Jiyin and took Dingtao below.
translations.idiomatic: He campaigned against Liu Yong in Jiyin and captured Dingtao.

sentences.zh: 初，純從攻王郎，墮馬折肩，時疾發，乃還詣懷宮。

translations.literal: At first, when Chun followed in attacking Wang Lang, he fell from a horse and broke his shoulder; at the time his illness broke out, and he thereupon returned to the Huai palace.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, in the war on Wang Lang, he had broken a shoulder in a fall from his horse; now an old malady flared and he withdrew to the Huai palace.

sentences.zh: 帝問：『卿兄弟誰可使者？

translations.literal: The Emperor asked: "Among your brothers, whom may be employed?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked, "Which of your brothers can I appoint to take your place?"

sentences.zh: 』純舉從弟植，於是使植將純營，純猶以前將軍從。

translations.literal: Chun nominated younger cousin Zhi; thereupon he ordered Zhi to lead Chun's camp, Chun still following as forward general.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun named his cousin Geng Zhi, who took command of his troops while Geng Chun kept the title of forward general in the field.

sentences.zh: 時真定王劉揚復造作讖記雲：『赤九之後，癭揚為主。

translations.literal: At that time the Prince of Zhending Liu Yang again forged prognostication texts saying: "After the Red Ninth, goiter Yang shall be lord."
translations.idiomatic: The Prince of Zhending, Liu Yang, was circulating forged prophecies: "After the house of the Red Ninth, the goitered Yang shall rule."

sentences.zh: 』揚病癭，欲以惑眾，與綿曼賊交通。

translations.literal: Yang was ill with a goiter and wished thereby to delude the masses, and communicated with the bandits of Mianman.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yang had a goiter and played on it to sway the crowd, while he colluded with the Mianman bandits.

sentences.zh: 建武二年春，遣騎都尉陳副、遊擊將軍鄧隆征揚，揚閉城門，不內副等。

translations.literal: In the spring of the second year of Jianwu he sent Colonel of Cavalry Chen Fu and General Who Roams to Attack Deng Long to summon Yang; Yang closed the city gates and would not admit Fu and the others.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of Jianwu 2 Guangwu sent Colonel Chen Fu and mobile corps general Deng Long against Liu Yang; Liu Yang barred the gates.

sentences.zh: 乃復遣純持節，行赦令於幽、冀，所過並使勞慰王侯。

translations.literal: He again sent Chun holding credentials, carrying out amnesty orders in You and Ji, and everywhere he passed also ordered comfort to princes and marquises.
translations.idiomatic: He then gave Geng Chun the staff of office to proclaim amnesty through You and Ji, comforting princes and marquises along the route.

sentences.zh: 密敕純曰：『劉揚若見，因而收之。

translations.literal: He secretly ordered Chun: "If Liu Yang should see you, then seize him."
translations.idiomatic: His secret instruction: seize Liu Yang if he agrees to a meeting."

sentences.zh: 』純從吏士百餘騎與副、隆會元氏，俱至真定，止傳舍。

translations.literal: Chun with over a hundred mounted clerks and soldiers joined Fu and Long at Yuanshi, together reached Zhending, and stopped at the relay inn.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun rode to Yuanshi with a hundred-odd horsemen, joined Chen Fu and Deng Long, and entered Zhending to the post station.

sentences.zh: 揚稱病不謁，以純真定宗室之出，遣使與純書，欲相見。

translations.literal: Yang claimed illness and did not pay visit; because Chun was of Zhending lineage stock, he sent an envoy with a letter to Chun, wishing to meet face to face.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yang pleaded illness but, knowing Geng Chun's Zhending roots, sent a letter asking for a private meeting.

sentences.zh: 純報曰：『奉使見王侯牧守，不得先詣，如欲面會，宜出傳舍。

translations.literal: Chun replied: "I have been ordered to see princes, marquises, and shepherds and guards; I may not call on them first; if you wish a face meeting, you should come out to the relay inn."
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun answered that envoys must wait on princes and governors, not the reverse—if Liu Yang wanted to talk, he should come to the station.

sentences.zh: 』時，揚弟臨邑侯讓及從兄細各擁兵萬餘人，揚自恃眾強而純意安靜，即從官屬詣之，兄弟並將輕兵在門外。

translations.literal: At that time Yang's younger brother the Marquis of Linyi Rang and elder cousin Xi each held troops over ten thousand; Yang relied on his mass strength while Chun's intention was quiet; he at once followed officials and retainers to visit him; the brothers together led light troops outside the gate.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yang's brother Rang, marquis of Linyi, and cousin Xi each commanded ten thousand men; confident in his numbers, Liu Yang thought Geng Chun harmless and walked in with a light escort while his brothers waited outside with picked troops.

sentences.zh: 揚入見純，純接以禮敬，因延請其兄弟，皆入，乃閉閤悉誅之，因勒兵而出。

translations.literal: Yang entered to see Chun; Chun received him with ritual respect, and for that reason extended invitation to his brothers; when all had entered, he thereupon closed the doors and executed them all to the last, then ordered troops and went out.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yang entered; Geng Chun greeted him with elaborate courtesy, invited the brothers inside, then bolted the doors and cut them all down before leading his men into the street.

sentences.zh: 真定震怖，無敢動者。

translations.literal: Zhending was shaken and terrified; none dared move.
translations.idiomatic: Zhending was paralyzed with fear; no one stirred.

sentences.zh: 帝憐揚、讓謀未發，並封其子，復故國。

translations.literal: The Emperor pitied that Yang and Rang's plot had not yet broken forth, and together enfeoffed their sons, restoring the former states.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor noted that Liu Yang and Liu Rang had not yet openly rebelled, so he enfeoffed their sons and restored their old titles out of mercy.

sentences.zh: 純還京師，因自請曰：『臣本吏家子孫，幸遭大漢復興，聖帝受命，備位列將，爵為通侯。

translations.literal: Chun returned to the capital and on his own requested: "I was originally a descendant of a clerk's household; fortunately I encountered great Han's restoration, the sage emperor received the mandate; I was fully arrayed among the ranked generals, my nobility is a full marquis."
translations.idiomatic: Back at the capital Geng Chun petitioned the throne: "I began as a clerk's son who caught the fortune of Han's restoration. Your majesty received Heaven's charge; I rose among the generals to a full marquis's rank."

sentences.zh: 天下略定，臣無所用誌，願試治一郡，盡力自效。

translations.literal: "The realm is in the main settled; I have no use in which to record my will; I beg to try governing one commandery and exhaust my strength in self-service."
translations.idiomatic: "The empire is nearly at peace, and I have little left to do as a soldier. Let me try a single commandery and spend whatever strength I have there."

sentences.zh: 』帝笑曰：『卿既治武，復欲修文邪？

translations.literal: The Emperor smiled and said: "You already administer martial affairs; do you again wish to cultivate civil affairs?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor smiled. "You have worn armor all your life—now you want to take up the ink brush?"

sentences.zh: 』乃拜純為東郡太守。

translations.literal: Thereupon he appointed Chun governor of Dongjun.
translations.idiomatic: He named Geng Chun governor of Dongjun.

sentences.zh: 時，東郡未平，純視事數月，盜賊清寧。

translations.literal: At that time Dongjun was not yet pacified; Chun assumed office several months, and bandits were cleared and at peace.
translations.idiomatic: Dongjun was still unsettled when he arrived, but within a few months he had cleared the banditry and restored calm.

sentences.zh: 四年，詔純將兵擊更始東平太守範荊，荊降。

translations.literal: In the fourth year he ordered Chun to lead troops to strike Gengshi's governor of Dongping Fan Jing; Jing surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of Jianwu he sent Geng Chun against Fan Jing, the Gengshi governor of Dongping, who capitulated.

sentences.zh: 進擊太山濟南及平原賊，皆平之。

translations.literal: He advanced to strike the bandits of Taishan, Jinan, and Pingyuan, and all pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: He then campaigned through Taishan, Jinan, and Pingyuan until every rebel band in the region was broken.

sentences.zh: 居東郡四歲，時發幹長有罪，純案奏，圍守之，奏未下，長自殺。

translations.literal: He dwelt in Dongjun four years; at that time the magistrate of Fagan county committed a crime; Chun investigated and memorialized, besieged and guarded him; before the memorial came down, the magistrate committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Four years into his tenure the magistrate of Fagan county broke the law; Geng Chun impeached him and had him confined pending the throne's ruling. Before the rescript came down the man took his own life.

sentences.zh: 純坐免，以列侯奉朝請。

translations.literal: Chun was sitting dismissed; as full marquis he attended court audience.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun was stripped of office for the affair but kept his marquis rank and continued to attend court as fengchaoqing.

sentences.zh: 從擊董憲，道過東郡，百姓老小數千隨東駕涕泣，雲『願復得耿君』。

translations.literal: He followed in striking Dong Xian; passing through Dongjun, several thousand old and young of the common people followed the eastern carriage weeping, saying, "We wish again to obtain Lord Geng."
translations.idiomatic: On the march against Dong Xian his column crossed Dongjun. Thousands of old and young trailed the imperial train in tears, crying, "Give us Lord Geng again."

sentences.zh: 帝謂公卿曰：『純年少被甲胄為軍吏耳，治郡乃能見思若是乎？』

translations.literal: The Emperor said to the dukes: "Chun in youth wore armor and helmet as a military clerk—governing a commandery could make him longed for like this?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor remarked to his ministers, "I knew Geng Chun only as the young officer in mail—who would have thought a turn as governor would win him such love?"

sentences.zh: 六年，定封為東光侯。

translations.literal: In the sixth year his fief was fixed as marquis of Dongguang.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 6 his title was fixed as marquis of Dongguang.

sentences.zh: 純辭就國，帝曰：『文帝謂周勃「丞相吾所重，君為我率諸侯就國」，今亦然也。

translations.literal: Chun declined and went to his state; the Emperor said: "Wen Di said to Zhou Bo, 'The chancellor is what I esteem; you for me lead the marquises to their states'—now it is likewise."
translations.idiomatic: When Geng Chun asked leave to retire to his fief, the emperor quoted Emperor Wen of Former Han to Zhou Bo: "The chancellor is indispensable to me, but you must still lead the marquises home to their estates." "The same holds now," he said.

sentences.zh: 』純受詔而去。

translations.literal: Chun received the edict and departed.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun bowed to the decree and left the capital.

sentences.zh: 至鄴，賜谷萬斛。

translations.literal: When he reached Ye, he was granted ten thousand hu of grain.
translations.idiomatic: At Ye he received an imperial gift of ten thousand hu of grain.

sentences.zh: 到國，吊死問病，民愛敬之。

translations.literal: When he arrived in his state, he condoled the dead and asked after illness; the people loved and revered him.
translations.idiomatic: Once home he mourned the dead and inquired after the sick; the people loved and honored him for it.

sentences.zh: 八年，東郡、濟陰盜賊群起，遣大司空李通、橫野大將軍王常擊之。

translations.literal: In the eighth year bandits of Dongjun and Jiyin rose in groups; he sent Grand Minister of Works Li Tong and Grand General Who Sweeps the Wilderness Wang Chang to strike them.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 8 rebels erupted across Dongjun and Jiyin; the court dispatched Grand Minister of Works Li Tong and Grand General Who Sweeps the Wilderness Wang Chang against them.

sentences.zh: 帝以純威信著於衛地，遣使拜太中大夫，使與大兵會東郡。

translations.literal: The Emperor because Chun's awe and trust were manifest in Wei territory sent an envoy to appoint him grand counselor of the palace and ordered him to join the great army at Dongjun.
translations.idiomatic: Because Geng Chun's name still commanded fear and trust on the Wei plain, the emperor named him grand counselor of the palace and sent him to coordinate with the main host in Dongjun.

sentences.zh: 東郡聞純入界，盜賊九千餘人皆詣純降，大兵不戰而還。

translations.literal: When Dongjun heard Chun had entered the border, over nine thousand bandits all came to Chun to surrender; the great army returned without battle.
translations.idiomatic: Word that Geng Chun had crossed the commandery line brought more than nine thousand outlaws to his camp to yield; the expeditionary army marched home without a fight.

sentences.zh: 璽書復以為東郡太守，吏民悅服。

translations.literal: An imperial writ again made him governor of Dongjun; officials and people were pleased and submitted.
translations.idiomatic: A second edict restored him as governor of Dongjun, to the delight of every clerk and farmer under his rule.

sentences.zh: 十三年，卒官，謚曰成侯。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year he died in office; his posthumous title was marquis of Cheng.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office in Jianwu 13 and received the posthumous epithet Cheng, "Accomplished."

sentences.zh: 子阜嗣。

translations.literal: His son Fu succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Geng Fu inherited the marquisate.

sentences.zh: 植後為輔威將軍，封威邑侯。

translations.literal: Zhi later became general who aids awe, enfeoffed as marquis of Weiyi.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Zhi later rose to general who aids awe and marquis of Weiyi.

sentences.zh: 宿至代郡太守，封遂鄉侯。

translations.literal: Su reached governor of Dai commandery, enfeoffed as marquis of Suixiang.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Su became governor of Dai and marquis of Suixiang.

sentences.zh: 訢為赤眉將軍，封著武侯，從鄧禹西征，戰死雲陽。

translations.literal: Xin became Red Eyebrows general, enfeoffed as marquis of Zhuwu, followed Deng Yu on the western campaign, and died in battle at Yunyang.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Xin ended as a Red Eyebrows general and marquis of Zhuwu; he followed Deng Yu west and fell at Yunyang.

sentences.zh: 凡宗族封列侯者四人，關內侯者三人，為二千石者九人。

translations.literal: In all, of the lineage group those enfeoffed as full marquises were four persons, as marquises within the Pass three persons, who served as two thousand shi were nine persons.
translations.idiomatic: The clan produced four full marquises, three marquises within the Pass, and nine men who held rank at two thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 阜徙封莒鄉侯，永平十四年，坐同族耿歙與楚人顏忠辭語相連，國除。

translations.literal: Fu was moved in fief to marquis of Juxiang; in the fourteenth year of Yongping, because same-lineage Geng She was connected in testimony with the Chu man Yan Zhong, the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Fu's fief shifted to Juxiang; in Yongping 14 it was stripped after kinsman Geng She was implicated with the Chu defendant Yan Zhong.

sentences.zh: 建初二年，肅宗追思純功，紹封阜子盱為高亭侯。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianchu the Suzong recalled Chun's merit and continued the line, enfeoffing Fu's son Xu as marquis of Gaoting.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 2 Emperor Zhang remembered Geng Chun's service and renewed the line, enfeoffing Geng Fu's son Geng Xu as marquis of Gaoting.

sentences.zh: 盱卒，無嗣，帝復封盱弟騰。

translations.literal: When Xu died there was no heir; the Emperor again enfeoffed Xu's younger brother Teng.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Xu died without sons, so the throne passed the title to his brother Geng Teng.

sentences.zh: 卒，子忠嗣。

translations.literal: When he died, his son Zhong succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Geng Teng died, his son Geng Zhong inherited.

sentences.zh: 忠卒，孫緒嗣。

translations.literal: When Zhong died, his grandson Xu succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Geng Zhong died, the marquisate passed to his grandson.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：任、邳識幾，嚴城解扉。

translations.literal: The praise says: Ren and Pi discerned the turning point; stern cities lifted their bars.
translations.idiomatic: The historian's summation: Ren Guang and Pi Tong read the moment aright, and cities that had barred their gates threw them open.

sentences.zh: 委佗還旅，二守焉依。

translations.literal: Easy paced the returning army on which two guardians it leaned.
translations.idiomatic: The beaten column limped homeward only because two steadfast governors still held for Han.

sentences.zh: 純、植義發，奉兵佐威。

translations.literal: Chun and Zhi, righteousness sprang forth; offering arms they aided awesome might.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun and Liu Zhi kindled duty in their clans and put spears at the emperor's service.
